description: The Islamic Revolutionary Courts (Dadgah-ha-ye Enqelab-e Eslami) were established in February 1979 immediately after the [Iranian Revolution](/events/iranian-revolution-1979) to try individuals accused of crimes against the revolution, including former officials of the Pahlavi regime, political dissidents, and those charged with "corruption on earth" (mofsed-e fel-arz) or "enmity against God" (moharebeh). Initially presided over by Sadegh Khalkhali under the authority of [Ruhollah Khomeini](/people/ruhollah-khomeini), the courts conducted summary trials with minimal due process. The courts remain an active part of Iran's [judiciary](/orgs/judiciary-of-iran), handling cases involving national security, narcotics, and political offences.
editorial_notes: Still active as of 2026. Handles political cases, drug offences, and national security cases.
